title: BMS makes a Beeline, bringing 5 assets to biotech's $300M precision immunology debut
snippet: Leading Beeline Medicines’ pipeline is afimetoran, which is in Phase 2 development for systemic lupus erythematosus with data expected later this year.

title: Takeda continues to prune partnerships, cuts ties with mRNA-targeting Veritas In Silico
snippet: After divorcing Denali Therapeutics earlier this month, Takeda is now splitting up with Veritas In Silico, pulling back from a partnership that advanced novel small-molecule drugs targeting mRNA.

title: Lilly’s new obesity pill linked to ‘serious’ safety signals, FDA requests more data
snippet: The FDA is asking Eli Lilly to submit cardiovascular and liver safety data from an ongoing Phase 3 trial of Foundayo by July.

title: J&J targets $100B revenue, Replimune rebuffed again and a “Revolution” in pancreatic cancer

title: Could biotech layoffs push life sciences talent to go global?
snippet: Amidst rising layoffs in key biotech hubs, global demand for specialized life sciences talent is driving a more borderless, distributed model of scientific work.

title: 5 biopharma M&A deals where the workforce was the prize
snippet: Gilead, AstraZeneca and Vertex have acquired more than just a therapeutic asset in recent deals. BioSpace takes a look at five recent transactions where the staff was the real centerpiece.

title: Deep dive: Checking in with the FDA
snippet: BioSpace looks back at 2025 and where the FDA is going in 2026.

title: FDA bolsters bespoke therapy framework with new draft safety guidelines
snippet: The draft guidance supports the agency’s new pathway designed to speed up the development of custom gene therapies.

title: 50 employees impacted as Astellas closes Universal Cells' Seattle office
snippet: By closing the Universal Cells Seattle location, Astellas is reportedly consolidating cell therapy, gene therapy and oncology research at its South San Francisco, California, and Westborough, Massachusetts, sites.

title: Bonus: Q1 2026 Job Market Update
snippet: In this bonus episode, BioSpace’s Vice President of Marketing ⁠Chantal Dresner⁠ and Careers Editor ⁠Angela Gabriel⁠ take a look at Q1 job market performance and what it signals for the coming months.

title: With nearly a quarter billion in Q1, J&J targets $100B revenue in 2026
snippet: With robust sales performance from oncology darling Darzalex and immunology superstar Tremfya, Johnson & Johnson is “off to a fast start in 2026,” CEO Joaquin Duato told investors on Tuesday.

title: Lilly investing billions to prepare for overseas oral GLP-1 launches
snippet: With many overseas patients preferring orals to injectables, Eli Lilly has filed for approval of orforglipron in more than 40 countries and is building manufacturing capacity to support the obesity drug.

title: Lilly wants to bridge cancer care gap with $300M ADC biotech buy
snippet: Eli Lilly is putting its obesity windfall to work again, striking a new deal to acquire CrossBridge Bio, a small Texas biotech known for its cancer tech.

title: On steadier ground, pharma heads into Q1 earnings with deals top of mind
snippet: During the pharma earnings season, which begins on Tuesday, Novo Nordisk will report the first revenue numbers from an oral GLP-1 medicine, while other companies are expected to address the FDA, drug pricing and Trump’s new tariffs.

title: Travere’s Filspari overcomes Phase 3 fail to become first drug for rare kidney disease
snippet: The FDA has greenlit Travere Therapeutics’ Filspari as the only available treatment for focal segmental glomerulosclerosis despite the drug’s loss to Sanofi’s Avapro in a Phase 3 study.

title: Kailera eyes record $533M+ IPO to play in competitive obesity space with Chinese drugs
snippet: Kailera Therapeutics is advancing a pipeline of obesity drugs, led by the GLP-1/GIP dual agonist ribupatide, which the biotech is developing both as an injectable and as a pill.

title: FDA asks 2,200 drug developers to make trial results public, ramping up transparency push
snippet: Some 30% of clinical trials that are mandated to report their findings have not posted results to clinicaltrials.gov, the federal government’s public repository for studies, according to internal data from the FDA.

title: Adopting a patient-centric approach is a competitive advantage
snippet: Bringing patients into the drug development process early has proven to save time, money and resources.

title: Diabetic Neuropathy: From Etiopathogenesis to Integrative Therapeutic Strategies with Traditional Chinese Medicine.
snippet: Diabetic neuropathy is a common and disabling complication of diabetes leading to numbness, pain, sensory loss and reduced quality of life. This review summarizes its diagnosis, epidemiology, genetic basis, pathophysiological mechanisms and current treatment strategies, with a focus on the role of traditional Chinese medicine (TCM). Its current common diagn...

title: The role of exercise-induced myokines in cancer-associated inflammation and immune regulation: mechanisms and prospects.
publicationName: Frontiers in sports and active living
journalName: Frontiers in sports and active living
snippet: Skeletal muscle is increasingly recognized as an endocrine organ, and contraction-induced myokines are viewed as key mediators of the systemic benefits of exercise. This review synthesizes evidence on the roles and molecular mechanisms of major myokines-including IL-6, irisin, SPARC, oncostatin M (OSM), and decorin-in exercise-associated modulation of cance...

title: Triglyceride-glucose-Body Mass Index is linked to papillary thyroid carcinoma odds in China: a retrospective study.
snippet: The incidence of thyroid cancer is gradually increasing year by year, with papillary thyroid carcinoma being the predominant type of thyroid cancer. Exploring the risk factors for papillary thyroid carcinoma is crucial for predicting and treating papillary thyroid carcinoma. This research investigated the correlation between the odds of papillary thyroid ca...

title: Discovery of cerebrospinal fluid biomarkers for different dementias using mass spectrometry-based proteomics.
snippet: Common forms of dementia include Alzheimer's disease (AD), dementia with Lewy bodies (DLB), and frontotemporal dementia (FTD). Disease specific biomarkers are needed for differential disease diagnosis. We performed cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) mass spectrometry proteomics on three cohorts ( = 110, = 112, = 78) including AD, DLB, F...

title: Identification of an m7G-Related lncRNA Signature for Prognostic Prediction and Immune Landscape Characterization in Early Gastric Cancer.
snippet: While early gastric cancer (EGC) is generally associated with a favorable prognosis, its clinical outcomes are notably heterogeneous. Unlike advanced gastric cancer requiring uniform intensive treatment, EGC lacks robust biomarkers to identify high-risk patients for personalized therapy. Although both N7-methylguanosine (m7G) modification and long non-codin...
